portfolio.name: NexaCore
portfolio.tagline: Workflow automation for ops-heavy B2B teams

section1_vision.oneLiner: We eliminate the 3 hours per day ops teams waste on manual handoffs.
section1_vision.problem: Mid-market B2B companies with 20–200 employees lose $180k/yr per ops person to workflow friction. The tools exist. The connections between them don't.
section1_vision.solution: NexaCore is the connective tissue. Drop-in integrations + AI-powered routing = zero-touch workflow automation in under 2 weeks.
section1_vision.whyNow: The SMB automation wave is 18 months behind enterprise. The window to own this segment is open now.

section2_market.som: $12M — reachable in 24 months with current GTM

competitors.name: Zapier
competitors.gap: Built for individuals, not ops teams. No role-based access.

competitors.name: Monday.com
competitors.gap: Project management, not process automation. Different buyer.

competitors.name: Make (Integromat)
competitors.gap: Too technical for ops teams. Requires developer setup.

moats: Deep ops team integrations (not just surface-level APIs)
Role-based workflow logic — Zapier can't do this
distribution: Outbound to ops directors at 20–200 person companies
section5_winStrategy.unfairAdvantage: Founder ran ops at 3 companies — knows the exact pain from inside

revisions.change: Added unit economics after first 10 customers
revisions.trigger: Real CAC data came in 4x higher than projected

revisions.change: Pivoted ICP from B2C freelancers to B2B ops teams
revisions.trigger: 3 B2B inbound leads with 8x higher LTV signals

revisions.change: Rewrote GTM section for B2B motion. Updated pricing.
revisions.trigger: Founder post-mortem on Q4 B2C attempt

experiments.hypothesis: LinkedIn outbound will outperform email cold outreach for ops directors
Free onboarding call increases 90-day retention by >20%

lessons: B2C CAC in SaaS is brutal without a viral loop. We didn't have one.
B2B buyers move slower but stay longer. The math is better.
Our best feature is the one we almost cut: role-based workflow routing.

portfolio.name: Velocity
portfolio.tagline: Performance coaching platform for ambitious professionals

section1_vision.oneLiner: The operating system for high-performance professionals who don't have a coach.
section1_vision.problem: Top performers waste 40% of their potential on bad habits, poor recovery, and decision fatigue. Executive coaches cost $500+/hr and aren't available 24/7.
section1_vision.solution: Velocity is an AI-powered performance coach — tracks your biometrics, calendar, and decisions, then gives you a personalized daily optimization brief.
section1_vision.whyNow: Wearable adoption crossed 40% of professionals. The data exists. Nobody is synthesizing it intelligently yet.

competitors.name: BetterUp
competitors.gap: Human coaching, expensive, not personalized enough

competitors.name: Headspace
competitors.gap: Mental wellness only, no performance integration

moats: Proprietary performance model trained on user data over time
Network effects: team mode unlocks when 3+ members at same company
distribution: Content-led: founder sharing performance insights on LinkedIn/Twitter
Community: founder-led Slack community for performance-focused professionals
section5_winStrategy.unfairAdvantage: Smoke (Sheldon) IS the product — his logic system is the coaching framework

experiments.hypothesis: Weekly group calls increase retention vs async-only

lessons: Beta users don't churn when they feel seen. Personalization is the product.

portfolio.name: Blaze
portfolio.nextMilestone: Decide: pivot or shut down by March 15th

section1_vision.oneLiner: Fiverr for senior specialists — vetted, fast, expensive, worth it.
section1_vision.problem: Companies need expert help for 10-40 hours, not 6 months. Finding vetted fractional talent takes 3+ weeks and costs $20k in recruiter fees.
section1_vision.solution: Blaze is a curated marketplace with pre-vetted specialists available within 48 hours.
section1_vision.whyNow: Post-layoffs, 3.2M senior professionals went fractional. Supply is there. Demand-side discovery is broken.

competitors.name: Fiverr Pro
competitors.gap: Race to the bottom on pricing

moats: Curation — quality over quantity in specialist roster
distribution: LinkedIn outbound to HR/ops buyers — not working at current volume
section5_winStrategy.unfairAdvantage: None identified yet — this is the problem

revisions.change: Pivoted from individual freelancers to specialists-only

lessons: Marketplace cold starts require demand-side subsidy. We didn't plan for this.
Quality curation is a moat — but only after you have demand.
3 months without traction = decision point. Don't extend out of hope.
